meaning: n.不连贯,语无伦次,[物]无内聚力
examples: The main clinical manifestations were consciousness disorder accompanied by incoherence of thinking, amnesia, visual hallucination and behavioral disturbance.
Yet Germany too is guilty of incoherence.
In times of crisis, you get a public reaction that is incoherence on stilts.
What could have prompted such an onslaught of emotion, ranging from unadulterated excitement to utter incoherence?
Their inconsistency, even incoherence, over getting creditors to pay has done much to spread contagion.
